message:
fix(daemon,logger): 5 review findings — state write ordering, executor reload, channel semantics, retention, walker doc

SelfMonitor: move last_write update to AFTER successful write_state_atomic(),
  preventing stale state.json for an entire write_interval on I/O failure.

Executor: share dry_run/max_batch_size/min_score via Arc<Atomic*> between
  main thread and executor. Config reload (SIGHUP) now propagates these
  safety-critical settings without respawning the thread.

Scanner channel: reduce capacity 16→2 to bound data staleness. Fix misleading
  "latest-wins" comment — try_send defers newest request on full channel,
  retried on next monitor tick. Add eprintln diagnostic when deferred.

SQLite: add prune_pressure_history() and prune_activity_log() with 30-day
  retention. Called every ~3600 events from the logger thread to prevent
  unbounded table growth (was ~2-4 GB/year without pruning).

Walker: fix doc comment that falsely claimed inode deduplication — the walker
  relies on max_depth for cycle protection, not inode tracking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
